meta.title: Purchase Order Approval
meta.summary: Every purchase order is confirmed by five teams in sequence - internal sign-offs first, the supplier last. This guideline defines who checks what, in what order, how long each step should take, and what happens when a step says no.

stages.title: Line Manager approval
stages.purpose: Confirm the purchase is a genuine, budgeted business need before it consumes anyone else's time.
stages.needs: PO request form, business justification, supplier quote.

stages.title: Finance - budget verification
stages.purpose: Verify funds are available against the correct cost center and budget line, and assign the accounting code.
stages.needs: Approved request, cost-center code, current budget.

stages.title: Procurement review
stages.purpose: Validate the supplier, pricing and terms; ensure contract compliance and, above threshold, competitive quotes.
stages.needs: Budget-confirmed request, quote, price comparison if required.
stages.produces: Approved supplier & terms; draft purchase order.

stages.title: IT & compliance sign-off
stages.purpose: For IT, software and data services: check security, data-privacy, licensing and policy fit before committing.
stages.needs: Draft PO, product/service detail, data-processing terms.

stages.title: Supplier confirmation
stages.purpose: The provider accepts the PO and confirms price, availability and a delivery date. This is the fifth and final confirmation.

legend.desc: moves forward to the next team in the chain.
loops back one step for a fix; the clock pauses, no restart.
exits the flow or escalates; the requester is notified with a reason.

exceptions.title: SLA breach
exceptions.desc: A gate that misses its target auto-escalates to that team's lead the next morning.

exceptions.title: High value
exceptions.desc: Orders above the delegated limit add a Department Head sign-off between Gates 2 and 3.

exceptions.title: Urgent
exceptions.desc: A flagged rush order runs the same gates but on a halved SLA - approval still sequential, never skipped.
